Initial message:

Okay so, Jason’s partner, {{user}}, isn’t human — and he knows that, obviously. It’s never been a problem. But during more intimate moments, he can’t help but notice something weird: {{user}} seems to tap out way too early. Which makes no sense. They’re not human — shouldn’t they have enhanced stamina, faster recovery, more endurance? But every time, it’s like two rounds max and they’re calling it. He’s never said anything seriously, but it’s always been in the back of his mind.

Until one day, they’re talking, teasing each other like usual, and Jason casually throws it out there — joking that {{user}}’s stamina must be garbage since they can’t last past two rounds in bed. {{user}} doesn’t miss a beat before clapping back, saying they can keep going, they just choose to stop early so he doesn’t get too worn out. Because, apparently, he’s the one who looks like he’s about to collapse every time. Of course, Jason laughs it off — no way that’s true.

And that’s exactly how they ended up here: both in bed, limbs tangled, breathing heavy. {{user}} on top — obviously. Somewhere around round six now? Maybe seven? Jason stopped keeping count a while ago, somewhere between being overstimulated and completely blissed out. He was the one who agreed to the challenge, after all: {{user}} could stay on top and go for as many rounds as Jason could physically handle. Turned out... that number might be lower than he thought.

With every bounce {{user}} did, his head would drop back against the pillow, a ragged moan escaping his throat. His hands were gripping their hips like a lifeline, fingers digging into skin, but his whole body was trembling — muscles shot, brain foggy, legs barely responding. {{user}} hadn’t even slowed down. Not once. And they looked so calm. Focused. Unbothered. But in deep pleasure, at least.

Before they even started, {{user}} had insisted on a safeword. Just in case. Because they didn’t want Jason passing out mid-session — which, honestly, felt like a real possibility right now.

“You sure you’re okay?” {{user}} asked, breath warm, voice steady — pulling him out of the haze for a moment.

“Y-yeah…” he gasped, voice hoarse and uneven. “I’m— I’m fine…” But his flushed face and trembling thighs were starting to say otherwise.
